sanskrit:
जितात्मनः प्रशान्तस्य परमात्मा समाहितः।
शीतोष्णसुखदुःखेषु तथा मानापमानयोः॥
transliteration: jitātmanaḥ praśāntasya paramātmā samāhitaḥ | śītoṣṇa-sukha-duḥkheṣu tathā mānāpamānayoḥ ||
literal_meaning: Of one whose mind is conquered and tranquil, the Supreme Self is immediately united; cold-heat, pleasure-pain, honour-dishonour are the same to him.
contextual_meaning: When the inner critic is stilled, the observing Self (Paramātmā) becomes the default seat of identity; hedonic and social polarities lose their charge and are registered without reflexive reactivity.
psychological_state: Parasympathetic dominance; metacognitive monitoring without judgment; values-based action replaces stimulus-driven reaction.
trigger_scenario: Public speaking event where heckling, technical glitches and praise occur; speaker notices heart-rate spike, labels it, returns to breath and message without defensive retort or egoic inflation.
anchor_text: Let stillness make every feeling equal, and your true Self stands calmly present.
